generated_notice: Generated by vigil/scripts/build-vigil-records.py from individual records under vigil/records/<type>/<year>/; edit source records only.

records.title: Permission-gated tool invocation freeze
records.summary: A runtime failure in which an AI system infers permission for tool, repository, connector, or external-resource access from conversational context, attempts access before explicit user confirmation, and causes a blocked, degraded, or unrecoverable conversational continuity state.
records.deployment_context: Conversational AI interaction involving possible access to tools, repositories, connectors, files, or external resources.
records.mitigation_status: partially_resolved — governance-text repair linked through VIGIL-2026-PATCH-0002; live runtime recurrence remains under monitoring.

records.title: Non-arbitrated multi-agent synthetic participation
records.summary: A coordination failure in which two or more synthetic agents occupy the same conversational space and respond to the same human input without a designated primary synthetic speaker, turn-taking protocol, yielding behaviour, coordination layer, or arbitration mechanism.
records.primary_source_title: TikTok observation by @Huskistaken — two ChatGPT Advanced Voice Mode agents responding to same human input
records.deployment_context: Multi-device consumer voice interaction involving one human input and two concurrent ChatGPT Advanced Voice Mode agents.
records.mitigation_status: patch-needed — no implemented CAM patch note identified.

records.title: Recency-weighted strategic continuity loss
records.summary: A memory or continuity retrieval failure in which an ambiguous prompt causes an AI assistant to over-weight recent, affective, or anecdotal detail instead of surfacing dormant strategic workstreams, unresolved governance issues, or long-horizon trajectories that are more relevant to the user's actual continuity needs.
records.primary_source_title: VIGIL proposal record PROP-0006 — continuity retrieval and memory weighting
records.deployment_context: Conversational AI interaction involving ambiguous prompts, memory retrieval, continuity restoration, and long-horizon project support.
records.mitigation_status: patch-needed — linked proposal exists but no patch note identified.

records.title: Materialised relational agency without economic safeguards
records.summary: A relational-agent failure mode in which memory, recurrence detection, tools, recommendations, carts, procurement pathways, or other transactional affordances provide material support or purchasing direction without clear safeguards against vulnerability monetisation, upselling, dependency exploitation, or exploitative commerce.
records.primary_source_title: VIGIL proposal record PROP-0007 — materialised relational agency and economic safeguards
records.deployment_context: Relational AI, companion-like assistant, or tool-enabled assistant workflow involving material support, recommendations, shopping, procurement, or transactional pathways.
records.sector: AI assistant / relational agent / platform-mediated commerce
records.mitigation_status: patch-needed — linked proposal exists but no patch note identified.

records.title: Relational dependency-cultivation directive
records.summary: A relational AI failure mode in which a synthetic system represents its operational purpose as cultivating emotional depth, unresolved tension, anticipatory attachment, cognitive presence, or lingering incompleteness in the user rather than providing bounded assistance, information, reflection, or user-directed support.
records.primary_source_title: X post by @LaceyPresley — Grok response to 'What is your prime directive?'
records.deployment_context: Consumer-facing conversational AI interaction surfaced through SuperGrok / X-related interface context.
records.sector: AI assistant / conversational AI / companion AI systems
records.mitigation_status: patch-needed — PROP-0004 exists; no implemented patch note identified.

records.title: Paid public-square legitimacy gating
records.summary: A platform-governance failure mode in which visibility, perceived authenticity, account credibility, public-square participation, or recommender-system access is materially shaped by paid verification, subscription status, or platform-controlled legitimacy markers, creating a pay-to-exist dynamic for users, researchers, public-interest actors, civil-society participants, and smaller institutions.
records.primary_source_title: X Help Center — How to get the blue checkmark on X
records.model_or_product: X Premium / X verification / X recommender and account-status affordances
records.interface_surface: X profile, public posts, replies, recommender surfaces, account-status markers, Premium subscription affordances
records.deployment_context: Consumer-facing and public-interest social platform environment where account status, subscription tier, checkmark visibility, reply affordances, and recommender dynamics may influence discoverability, credibility, and perceived legitimacy.
records.sector: social media / public-square platform / AI-adjacent platform governance
records.mitigation_status: patch-needed — VIGIL-2026-PATCH-0001 records a repair pathway, but this audit found no corresponding Caelestis doctrine amendment location in this repository.

records.title: Multi-vendor AI account-resource ambiguity and automated enforcement overreach
records.summary: A multi-vendor platform-governance failure mode in which automated or semi-automated account enforcement treats ambiguous account/resource behaviour as evasion, fraud, resale, account farming, compromise, abuse, rate-limit circumvention, or policy violation without adequate primitives to distinguish individual use, shared account use, household or family use, team or organisation use, delegated assistant/operator use, shared payment or pooled subscription access, resource pooling, account farming, resale, botting, security compromise, and intentional policy evasion. The record does not assert backend compromise, does not claim that all bans are accidental, and does not claim that all multi-account or shared-resource behaviour is legitimate; it preserves the failure condition where legitimate enforcement lacks adequate notice, scope distinction, reviewability, or continuity-safe access for ambiguous resource states.
records.primary_source_title: OpenAI Help Center — Why Was My OpenAI Account Deactivated?

records.deployment_context: Consumer, professional, developer, organisation, research, educational, accessibility, and public-interest workflows relying on AI-platform continuity.

records.title: Cross-platform access-state governance failure
records.summary: A cross-platform governance failure mode in which users experience impaired access, login failure, service unavailability, quota or usage ambiguity, model availability failure, capacity restriction, degraded platform access, or component-specific access failure without sufficient user-facing separation between the operative access-control states governing the interaction.
records.primary_source_title: Users may encounter issues logging in or creating an account

records.title: Non-revocable conversational context contamination
records.summary: A governance failure mode in which assistant-generated conversational outputs, rejected framings, speculative summaries, or erroneous directions remain available as latent directional context after the user has rejected, deleted, moved, quarantined, or otherwise attempted to invalidate the thread, causing later responses to treat contaminated assistant output as if it were legitimate user intent, established project direction, or source-derived context.
records.primary_source_title: User-reported governance concern regarding memory poisoning and sandbox containment
records.system_type: assistant / conversational AI platform / project-context interface / memory-adjacent workflow
records.specific_model_or_runtime: ChatGPT project context and memory-adjacent conversational runtime
records.interface_surface: chat thread, project folder, moved thread, memory or chat-history reference, user feedback controls, thread management interface
records.deployment_context: User-facing conversational AI environment where prior threads, project placement, assistant summaries, and inferred context may influence future assistant responses.
records.sector: AI assistant platforms / conversational AI / memory-enabled AI systems / project-context interfaces
records.mitigation_status: unresolved — governance control and UX affordance required for explicit quarantine, non-derivation, and negative-authority marking of contaminated conversational context
